reason: Bitcoin has failed to hold key psychological levels ($70,000, then $68,000) and is experiencing significant downside pressure. Macroeconomic factors, such as a strong dollar, are contributing to weakness in risk assets. Furthermore, an investment firm warns of a potential 30% crash, and contagion from traditional finance (BlackRock fund cracking) is directly impacting crypto markets. The overall sentiment is highly bearish, indicating a period of sustained price decline.
headlines: Bitcoin slips below $68,000 heading into the weekend as dollar posts steepest weekly gain in a year
Bitcoin could crash by another 30% as four-year cycle gains strength, investment firm says
Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
